subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, EC, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI) + Lithuanian official (Statistics Lithuania, Bank of Lithuania) + Lithuanian independent press (LRT).
A strong-institution, low-debt EU/eurozone NATO frontline state; the dominant tail is frontline exposure to the Russia-Ukraine war.

Standout features: strong institutions, a fintech/high-tech niche economy, top-tier defence commitment, against a real Russia-border war tail and demographic decline. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone on security framing.

economic.notes: Low debt and high-value export niches, against a defense-driven rising deficit.

fiscal_state.notes: Low debt (~40% GDP) but on a clear upward path toward ~55% by 2030 as defence rises above 5% of GDP; a ~1.8% deficit and expansionary. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB is credible, with no national control. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The euro, the world's second reserve currency. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Eased but re-firming ~4.4% on energy/wages, moderating ahead. (EC; Bank of Lithuania)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and Nordic-owned, with the EU's top licensed-fintech hub under central-bank supervision. (IMF; Bank of Lithuania)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Strong export growth and high-value niches (lasers, biotech, fintech), EU-integrated; the lost Russia/Belarus market re-oriented. (OECD; EC)

institutional.notes: Strong, EU-standard institutions across the board.

rule_of_law.notes: A high WJP standing with EU-standard judicial independence. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Statistics Lithuania is credible and uncompromised. (EC; WJP)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable and highly digital, with an improving corruption standing. (TI 2025)

press_freedom.notes: Free (RSF ~14th) - a clear strength, with contested public-broadcaster-law amendments a watch. (RSF 2025; LRT)

political_social.notes: A stable democracy with a strong security consensus, against demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: A stable EU democracy with orderly transfers and a strong anti-Russia consensus, tempered by a 2025 corruption-resignation and a fragile coalition. (LRT; OSW)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong, with minor friction over expression-related bills. (WJP; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: An EU lawful framework with foreign residents up sharply, against Belarus-engineered border-migration pressure. (Statistics Lithuania; EC)

social_cohesion.notes: Cohesive, with a strong national/security consensus. (V-Dem; LRT)

demographics.notes: Very low fertility (~1.11) and heavy ageing, with an emigration legacy only recently stabilised by return migration. (Statistics Lithuania)

geopolitical.notes: Top-tier alliance value, squarely offset by frontline war exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO/EU frontline stalwart and the alliance's top defence spender (~5.4% of GDP), hosting a permanent German brigade and hardline on Russia/China. (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: Frontline to the Russia-Ukraine war - Kaliningrad/Suwalki-gap exposure, the Belarus border, and repeated drone incursions - a real elevated tail. (LRT; Washington Post)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Good infrastructure and energy independence, against medic emigration.

climate.notes: A Baltic high-latitude beneficiary near/mid-term (warming-driven agricultural/season upside, good readiness), with the gain easing long-term (AMOC/Baltic tail). (ND-GAIN; Burke et al.)

healthcare.notes: Universal and decent, with medic emigration and ageing strain. (OECD)

infrastructure.notes: Good and EU-funded, energy-independent from Russia, with the Baltic grid synced to continental Europe in 2025. (EC; Bank of Lithuania)

summary: A low-debt EU and eurozone democracy with strong, EU-standard institutions, high-value export niches (lasers, biotech, the EU's top fintech hub), a top-tier NATO defence commitment (~5.4% of GDP, hosting a permanent German brigade), and energy independence from Russia after the 2025 Baltic grid synchronisation. The dominant tail is geopolitical: frontline exposure to the Russia-Ukraine war via the Kaliningrad/Suwalki gap and the Belarus border, with repeated drone incursions. Very low fertility and ageing are the main structural drag, leaving a balanced outcome distribution.
